items.title: After watchdog slams understaffing, AI to vet Pentagon-backed professors’ China ties
items.snippet: AI’s confusion over the nature of DOD research partnerships may mask real espionage if humans are not the final judge of foreign influence, experts warn.

items.title: US Army turns to Ukraine-tested drones to counter Iranian UAV threat
items.snippet: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll told lawmakers the service has purchased 13,000 low-cost interceptor drones to counter Iranian-made Shahed drones.

items.title: US Army wants doctrine for landing helicopters on Arctic ice
items.snippet: The Army Corps of Engineers’ research and development component is working to disprove old assumptions about the risks of landing on frozen lakes.

items.title: L3Harris announces $1 billion expansion in Virginia
items.snippet: The expansion will more than double manufacturing space at the Virginia site, which currently spans 256,000 square feet.

items.title: Space Force’s 15-year vision calls for more personnel, simulators and survivability
items.snippet: A new Space Force vision document grapples with the future of space warfare and how the service can prepare for it.

items.title: US Air Force wants better infrared jammers for its combat rescue helicopters
items.snippet: The HH-60W, recently used to rescue downed pilots in Iran, lacks a capable infrared countermeasure system, Air Force Material Command said.

items.title: Baltic nations brace for impact of Iran war delaying US weapons shipments
items.snippet: Delays in ammunition deliveries could reopen a thorny question for Baltic HIMARS users: Would the U.S. permit non-Lockheed rockets to be loaded?

items.title: After 200 years scientists finally crack the “dolomite problem”
items.snippet: After two centuries of failed attempts, scientists have finally grown dolomite in the lab, cracking a long-standing geological puzzle. They discovered that the mineral’s growth stalls because of tiny ...

items.title: US Navy destroyer fires on cargo vessel attempting to sail to Iranian port
items.snippet: The USS Spruance "disabled Touska’s propulsion by firing several rounds from the destroyer’s 5-inch MK 45 Gun into Touska’s engine room," CENTCOM stated.

items.title: Air Force unit executes test of Anduril’s semiautonomous combat drone
items.snippet: Airmen operated Auduril's YFQ-44A jet-powered combat drone, which could someday fly alongside piloted fighters.

items.title: US to delay weapons deliveries to some European countries due to Iran war, sources say
items.snippet: Some European counterparts have been told that select U.S. weapons deliveries may be delayed as the Iran war continues to draw on weapons stocks.

items.title: France nears rocket artillery decision, plans ballistic missile by 2035
items.snippet: French defense leaders are treating the acquisition of a new rocket artillery weapon as priority for the land forces.
